candidate_sources_rejected: complex-fracture guideline as a routine follow-up source
fracture-specific surgical guidelines as complete coverage
rejection_reasons: The workflow is intended for non-complex follow-up, not complex trauma.
Patient leaflets are not clinician guidance.
No single fracture-site surgical guideline covers the broad workflow.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for selected non-complex child and adult fractures; not exact for complex, open, hip, spinal, pelvic, every operative, or every cast complication context.
setting_applicability: Partial: UK emergency and orthopaedic clinic guidance versus UAE outpatient follow-up documentation.
UAE_applicability: No direct UAE applicability is established; UAE official sources were searched and NICE NG38 requires UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG38, last reviewed 2025-06-23, was opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: NG38 remains current after the June 2025 surveillance review.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped non-complex fracture identity, pain assessment, and clinician-entered imaging review.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped selected weight-bearing, immobilisation, stability, and orthopaedic follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports selected orthopaedic follow-up, stability review, weight-bearing, cast or fixation context, and return review for non-improving symptoms in specified fracture populations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped questions, imaging continuity, rehabilitation, management-plan, return, and follow-up documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires structured fracture documentation, image and report continuity, honest information, questions, treatment expectations, rehabilitation, mobilisation, weight-bearing, management-plan communication, and documentation.

unresolved_source_gaps: Generic healing status, swelling, neurovascular and cast-tightness signs, surgical wound review, fixed four-to-six-week imaging, and all fracture-site-specific schedules remain unsupported.
The healing-fracture diagnosis label remains clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.

legacy_item_support_mappings.direct_relationship: The exact NICE scope and assessment sections support these non-complex fracture identity, pain, and clinician-entered image-review fields.
